title: synth_xilinx creates too many RAMB18E1 instances
body:
For the code below, the synth_xilinx pass is detecting the block ram correctly, but it is mapping to two RAMB18E1 but it should map to just one. I checked with vivado that this does in fact map to only 1 RAMB18E1 block.

```
=== reg_file ===

   Number of wires:                 11
   Number of wire bits:            112
   Number of public wires:           7
   Number of public wire bits:      76
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  2
     RAMB18E1                        2
```

```verilog
module reg_file(input wire clk,
                input wire wr_en,
                input wire [9:0] w_addr,
                input wire [9:0] r_addr,
                input wire [17:0] w_data,
                output reg [17:0] r_data,
                output reg [17:0] r_data2);

  reg [17:0] ram [0:1023];

  always @(posedge clk)
  begin
    r_data <= ram[r_addr];
  end

  always @(posedge clk)
  begin
    if (wr_en)
      ram[w_addr] <= w_data;
    r_data2 <= ram[w_addr];
  end

endmodule
```
code_blocks:
=== reg_file ===

   Number of wires:                 11
   Number of wire bits:            112
   Number of public wires:           7
   Number of public wire bits:      76
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  2
     RAMB18E1                        2
